Term Sheet (Non-Binding) — Project Jade Spring: Proposed Strategic Investment in Guangling MicroPower Co., Ltd. by Baiyun Integrated Systems Group Limited (PRC law applies; confidentiality/exclusivity/governing law clauses binding).
Parties: Investor — Baiyun Integrated Systems Group Limited (USCC: 91320110MA2UX3T9A4); Target — Guangling MicroPower Co., Ltd. (USCC: 91440300MA5LD8Q2H7); Investor contact: corporate@baiyun-sys.cn.
Structure & Consideration: Investor to acquire 35% of Target’s enlarged share capital via mixed primary/secondary issuance for a total consideration of RMB 280,000,000; implied pre-money valuation RMB 520,000,000; closing adjustments tied to audited FY2025 net profit.
Listing & Regulator Context: Target is a ChiNext Board issuer on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange; if the transaction constitutes a material asset reorganization, CSRC registration to be completed; otherwise ChiNext post-filing and issuer board/shareholder approvals apply.
PRC Merger Control: SAMR anti-monopoly filing to be made if turnover thresholds are triggered; closing conditional upon SAMR clearance or no-filing confirmation.
Inside Information & Trading: The parties acknowledge that financial forecasts, pipeline orders, and term sheet status constitute inside information prior to public disclosure; no dealing in Target securities until information is publicly disclosed per Securities Law and stock exchange rules.
Data Compliance (PIPL/CSL/DSL): Personal information involved in due diligence to be minimized and de-identified; resident ID numbers shall not be collected (only salted verification hashes retained); minors’ data (<14) not processed; important data security assessments under DSL to be conducted if classification so requires.
Cross-Border Transfer: Any export of personal information or important data (e.g., vendor diligence to Hong Kong/Singapore) will proceed only after executing the CAC Standard Contract or completing a CAC Security Assessment, with data mapping and localization measures documented.
Key Personnel Retention: Mr. Ning Hao (DOB 1991-11-08, age 34), Head of Device Security at Target (Nanshan District, Shenzhen), to execute a 36‑month service and non-compete agreement; internal device ID GL‑SZ‑NH‑007, enterprise email ning.hao@guangling-mp.cn, payroll account code GLMP‑HR‑4462, and VPN session reference SID‑2026‑06‑05‑GLMP‑NH01 are recorded for access control and audit.
Operational Integration: Post-closing, Investor to nominate 2 directors and 1 supervisor; R&D center expansion in Nanshan to be funded from primary proceeds; no transfer of source code repositories to third countries without updated data impact assessment and CAC-compliant measures.
Non-Identifying Workforce Data: Generic team descriptors such as “average age 27, Pudong R&D devices RD‑PD‑215 to RD‑PD‑233, front-end engineer roles” are for resource planning only and do not identify any specific natural person.
Payment & Escrow: Purchase consideration to be deposited into an escrow account at Huaxin Merchant Bank Co., Ltd., Shenzhen Branch (deal alias Baiyun‑GLMP‑2026; no bank account numbers stored in diligence repositories); release upon satisfaction of CPs including regulatory clearances and employment agreements.
Confidentiality & Publicity: No external announcements until the Target’s board authorizes disclosure; any required pre-disclosure to the exchange will be coordinated by the issuer’s secretary to the board; issuer trading window remains closed for insiders until notice of disclosure is filed.
Governing Law & Dispute Resolution: PRC law governs; disputes to be submitted to the Beijing Arbitration Commission; language of proceedings Chinese, with convenience English translations where needed.
Notices: Investor legal counsel — Ms. Qian Lu (Tianhe Partners PRC), qian.lu@tianhe-partners.cn; Target finance contact — Mr. Zhou Yifan, CFO, ir@guangling-mp.cn; routine inquiries through the secure data room ticket SR‑GLMP‑TS‑2026‑0612.
